evidence_policy: Only facts directly observed in listed proxy logs are marked observed. Inferences are marked predicted or hypothesis and must be verified by controlled captures.

evidence_sources.role: first FIR408 startup and idle capture
executed FIR408 config readback of blocks 0x00..0x1F
evidence_sources.result: 32 block responses saved; assembled data length confirmed as 1556 bytes

evidence_sources.result: 12 responses saved; each response has 52 data bytes and all data bytes are 0xFF

config readback after Out1 FIR mode

evidence_sources.result: 45 non-meter writes captured: 0x4F transfer control, 43 x 0x4E coefficient chunks totaling 2048 data bytes, and 0x5B 8-byte file name; selector 0x00 should now be treated as input-channel selector candidate InA rather than Out1

evidence_sources.role: sequential 0x56 probes after external impulse FIR upload
evidence_sources.result: offset 0 returned little-endian float32 bytes 00 00 80 3F followed by zeros; offsets 13 and 26 returned zeros; offset 39 returned partial zeros then FF; offsets 52 and above returned FF

evidence_sources.role: prediction sample: load external 512-tap impulse FIR file on InB
evidence_sources.result: 0x4F, all 43 x 0x4E chunks, and 0x5B used selector 0x01, confirming InB selector prediction

evidence_sources.role: prediction sample: load 512-tap two-tap FIR file on InA
evidence_sources.result: 0x4E chunk 0 data prefix was 3F 80 00 00 3F 00 00 00; 0x56 offset 0 readback prefix was 00 00 80 3F 00 00 00 3F

frame.length_semantics: payload byte at offset 2 counts command byte plus argument bytes

checksum.algorithm: xor over all bytes from DLE STX through DLE ETX inclusive, then append 1 checksum byte
checksum.note: Same framing/checksum model as the existing DSP408 proxy decoder; all frames in the first FIR408 log were valid.

frame.frame_detection: start at DLE STX, end at DLE ETX; frame detection does not rely on a separate outer frame length

record_layout_in_config_dump.source: assembled 0x24 responses from first FIR408 startup log

name_field.encoding: ASCII, zero padded when shorter than 8 bytes

notes: The record starts are inferred from repeated channel labels at exact fixed strides in a single startup dump.
Field semantics inside the records still require controlled GUI change plus readback diff.
Channel labels were found by assembling data bytes from the 0x24 read-block responses in block order.
Input label offsets are 138 bytes apart in this default dump. Output label offsets are 108 bytes apart from Out1 through Out8.

gain.note: Inherited from DspLib-408.json for static config decode of common DSP408 gain fields.

peq_frequency.note: Inherited from the decoded DSP408 PEQ/crossover frequency model and confirmed by FIR 0x4B HighPass/LowPass raw samples.

peq_q.note: Observed PEQ Q writes fit a logarithmic GUI control covering 0.40 .. 128.00 over raw 0..100.

peq_gain.note: PEQ gain writes follow the same observed 0.1 dB storage step model as GEQ gain across -12 dB .. +12 dB.

geq_gain.note: Inferred strongly from a full-range InA 20 Hz GEQ fader series whose observed raw values climbed up to 240 at the positive end and align with 0.1 dB storage steps across a -12 dB .. +12 dB GUI range.

release_mapping.note: raw values below 9 may exist at protocol/storage level but are outside the GUI-valid range if the GUI minimum is 10 ms

matrix_crosspoint_gain.note: Anchored by observed points 0 dB => 280, -1 dB => 270, -20 dB => 80, -28 dB => 64, -30 dB => 60 and -60 dB => 0. Current best-fit model uses 2 raw steps per dB from -60..-20 and 10 raw steps per dB from -20..0.

fir_tap_count.note: Observed raw 8 -> 512 and raw 9 -> 544 in FIR generator 0x4B writes.

observations: loading decoderPres (user slot U01) was observed as payload 00 01 02 20 01
loading pres3 (user slot U03) was observed as payload 00 01 02 20 03
a previous preset load was observed as payload 00 01 02 20 00
after preset load, the GUI immediately re-read blocks 0x00 through 0x1C using command 0x27
setting 12 unique channel names in the GUI emitted 12 sequential command 0x3D writes, one per channel, rather than one combined bulk write
the write order observed was Out8 down to InA using channel indices 0x0B down to 0x00
name bytes are ASCII and zero padded to 8 bytes

InB gain fader capture produced a write series with payloads such as 00 01 04 34 01 11 01 ... 00 01 04 34 01 90 01

scripted read-side decode for InD used payloads 00 01 04 34 03 18 01 and 00 01 04 34 03 DC 00 and confirmed the same command/channel pattern
for all observed input gain captures/tests, only payload offsets 5 and 6 changed, confirming a u16le gain value field after the channel byte

confirmed by scripted write 00 01 04 34 02 18 01 vs 00 01 04 34 02 DC 00 and direct diffReport on block 0x08
confirmed by scripted write 00 01 04 34 03 18 01 vs 00 01 04 34 03 DC 00 and direct diffReport on block 0x0B

scripted read-side decode for InD used payloads 00 01 03 35 03 00 and 00 01 03 35 03 01 and confirmed channel byte 0x03

decoderPres_predicted_all_outputs.rule: all outputs Out1..Out8 share the same PEQ baseline values in decoderPres; only the output channel byte changes

observations: command 0x33 was observed during PEQ Out1 GUI changes
payload byte 4 matched Out1 as 0x04
the byte before the final reserved-looking byte changed with filter type
the 16-bit field immediately before filter_type changed together with GUI Q behavior
during controlled frequency changes on Out1/PEQ1, the second 16-bit field after channel changed while gain, Q and type stayed constant
during controlled gain changes on Out1/PEQ1, the first 16-bit field after channel changed while frequency, Q and type stayed constant
for Out1/PEQ9, payload byte 5 was observed as 0x08, matching a zero-based band index for PEQ9
for Out1/PEQ9, gain, frequency, Q and filter type reused the same field order as Out1/PEQ1
for Out8/PEQ9 Low Shelf, the payload matched the Out1/PEQ9 Low Shelf structure exactly except for the output channel byte changing from 0x04 to 0x0B
the user reported that all outputs Out1..Out8 use the same decoderPres PEQ values

band_index_u8.note: observed as 0x00 for PEQ1 and 0x08 for PEQ9 on Out1; strongly indicates zero-based PEQ band numbering

gain_or_level_u16.note: for Out1/PEQ1 Peak, this field changed from 0x0078 at 0.0 dB to 0x0082 at +1.0 dB while frequency, Q and type stayed constant; the same 0x0078 to 0x0082 pattern was observed for Out1/PEQ9

frequency_u16.note: for Out1/PEQ1 Peak, this field stepped across values such as 0x001F, 0x0020, 0x0021 ... 0x004F during controlled frequency movement while gain, Q and type stayed constant; for Out1/PEQ9 Peak it stepped from 0x011D to 0x011E during a controlled frequency step

examples.0x000A: observed when switching to shelf filters with GUI auto-Q around 0.71

q_u8_or_u16le.note: current evidence suggests the logical Q value may fit in a single byte, but the frame field is still documented conservatively as the bytes immediately before filter_type

gain_u16le.note: observed clearly on InA PEQ8 and consistent with the PEQ command family

frequency_u16le.note: observed clearly on InA PEQ8 where the frequency field changed from 0x010E to 0x012C

predicted_all_inputs.rule: Input PEQ uses the same 0x33 payload family for InA..InD, with input channels 0x00..0x03 and zero-based PEQ band indices 0x00..0x07.

observations: automated read-decode for InA PEQ8 gain changed only block 0x02 offset 41
automated read-decode for InD PEQ1 gain changed only block 0x0A offset 19
automated read-decode for InD PEQ8 gain changed only block 0x0B offset 11
automated read-decode for InA PEQ8 frequency changed only block 0x02 offset 43
automated read-decode for InA PEQ8 q changed only block 0x02 offset 45
automated read-decode for InA PEQ8 filter_type changed only block 0x02 offset 46
automated read-decode for InA PEQ1 bypass changed block 0x1C offset 17 from 0x00 to 0x01
automated read-decode for InA PEQ8 bypass changed only block 0x1C offset 17
automated read-decode for InA PEQ8 bypass changed block 0x1C offset 17 from 0x01 to 0x81
InA crossover read anchors now exist at block 0x02 offset 47 for high-pass frequency and block 0x02 offset 49 for low-pass frequency
the observed PEQ8 gain anchor at 0x02:41 and the crossover anchors at 0x02:47/49 strongly support a compact contiguous InA PEQ read layout ending immediately before crossover storage

layout_hypothesis.rule: InA PEQ read storage is strongly predicted as 8 contiguous PEQ band records ending immediately before the InA crossover frequency fields. Each PEQ band uses 6 bytes: gain_u16le, frequency_u16le, q_raw_u8, filter_type_u8. Bypass is stored separately as a bitmask byte.

bit0.evidence: block 0x1C offset 17 changed 0x00 -> 0x01 when InA PEQ1 bypass was toggled

bit7.evidence: block 0x1C offset 17 changed 0x01 -> 0x81 when InA PEQ8 bypass was toggled while PEQ1 bypass remained set

bypass_prediction.note: the byte location is proven and endpoint bits PEQ1/PEQ8 are observed directly; intermediate bits PEQ2..PEQ7 remain strongly predicted as linear bit order

not_yet_proven: exact bypass bit-to-band mapping for PEQ2..PEQ7 inside block 0x1C offset 17
direct targeted read verification for InA PEQ1..PEQ7
full read layout for InB, InC, and InD input PEQ
read.status_note: InA input PEQ is practically complete for decoding purposes: PEQ8 is fully anchored, the compact PEQ record layout is strongly supported, and the PEQ bypass byte location plus endpoint bits for PEQ1 and PEQ8 are directly observed. Remaining uncertainty is limited to intermediate bypass bit mapping and direct confirmation of PEQ1..PEQ7 read slots.
read.cross_input_stride_note: InD gain anchors at PEQ1 (0x0A:19) and PEQ8 (0x0B:11) fit the same 6-byte per-band PEQ record model seen on InA, supporting a strong cross-input stride prediction for InB/InC/InD.

channel_selector.transport_note: The visible InA/InB/InC/InD selector buttons appear to be GUI-local context selection; no dedicated protocol write has been observed for the selector itself.

observations: InA GEQ 20 Hz band movement sent 00 01 05 48 00 00 F0 00
InB GEQ 20 Hz band movement sent 00 01 05 48 01 00 F0 00
InC GEQ 20 Hz band movement sent 00 01 05 48 02 00 F0 00
InA GEQ 20 KHz band movement sent 00 01 05 48 00 1E F0 00
InD GEQ 20 Hz band movement sent 00 01 05 48 03 00 00 00
The only observed protocol differences between the InA 20 Hz and InA 20 KHz captures were the band index byte changing from 0x00 to 0x1E while the gain bytes stayed the same
The only observed protocol differences between the InA 20 Hz and InD 20 Hz captures were the input channel byte changing from 0x00 to 0x03 while the band index stayed at 0x00
No dedicated GEQ channel-selector write has been observed; the selected input channel appears to be encoded directly in each 0x48 band-gain write

A full-range InA 20 Hz GEQ series changed only payload offset 6/7 and produced raw values [2, 6, 8, 9, 12, 23, 29, 39, 47, 56, 67, 73, 82, 94, 106, 114, 141, 147, 152, 191, 213, 235, 240], strongly suggesting a 0.1 dB storage step over the -12 dB .. +12 dB GUI range with raw 240 at the positive end

predicted_all_inputs.rule: GEQ uses the same 0x48 write layout for InA..InD; the input channel byte changes linearly from 0x00 to 0x03 and the band index is a zero-based 0..30 selector for the 31 visible bands.

predicted_all_band_writes.rule: For every GEQ band write, only the input channel byte, zero-based band index, and gain_u16le value vary. All 31 bands reuse the same 0x48 payload shape.

layout_hypothesis.rule: Input GEQ read storage is linear per input and per band. Each band uses one u16le. Bands advance by +2 bytes across usable block offsets 5..53, then wrap to the next block at offset 5. Input changes follow a +3 blocks / -10 bytes start-offset stride.

observations: Automated read-decode for InA GEQ 20 Hz changed only block 0x00 offset 37 when written from raw 0 to raw 240
Automated read-decode for InA GEQ 1 KHz changed only block 0x01 offset 21 when written from raw 0 to raw 240
Automated read-decode for InA GEQ 10 KHz changed only block 0x01 offset 41 when written from raw 0 to raw 240
Automated read-decode for InA GEQ 20 KHz changed only block 0x01 offset 47 when written from raw 0 to raw 240
Automated read-decode for InD GEQ 20 Hz changed only block 0x09 offset 7 when written from raw 0 to raw 240
not_yet_proven: direct targeted read verification for InB and InC GEQ bands
full multi-input verification of the predicted GEQ band layout beyond the observed anchor points

observations: for Out1 High Pass Bypass, payloads 00 01 05 32 04 00 00 14 and 00 01 05 32 04 00 00 00 were observed
for Out1 High Pass Frequency, command 0x32 was observed repeatedly with output channel byte 0x04 and changing u16 values in bytes 5 and 6 while the last byte stayed 0x00
for Out8 High Pass Bypass, payloads 00 01 05 32 0B 00 00 12 and 00 01 05 32 0B 00 00 00 were observed
earlier crossover runs were accidentally performed on Out8; those showed the same command structure with channel byte 0x0B
for InA High Pass Frequency, payload 00 01 05 32 00 2C 01 00 was observed
for InA High Pass Bypass, payload 00 01 05 32 00 2C 01 13 was observed
for InA High Pass slope, payload 00 01 05 32 00 2C 01 01 was observed for one slope endpoint and payload 00 01 05 32 00 2C 01 14 was observed for the opposite slope endpoint

Out8 High Pass slope/family was measured completely by moving the GUI selector from bottom to top while keeping frequency fixed at raw 0x000D; the final mode byte ran through every value from 0x01 to 0x14 without gaps
Out8 High Pass bypass was then toggled from OFF to ON while the selected slope remained fixed, and the write became 00 01 05 32 0B 0D 00 00; this establishes raw mode 0x00 as bypass ON for Out8 HP

the user later confirmed that input crossover selectors follow the same write-side pattern as the outputs; the crossover model is therefore treated as common for InA..InD and Out1..Out8, with only the channel byte changing

because all channels share the same command structure and only the channel index byte changes, the fully measured crossover HP selector pattern is treated as the common write rule for InA..InD and Out1..Out8: raw 0x00 means bypass ON and raw 0x01..0x14 map to crossover_slopes in enum order

frequency_u16.note: bytes 5 and 6 changed as a little-endian value during controlled HP frequency changes and fit the same logarithmic 19.7 Hz .. 20.16 kHz model used for PEQ frequency

observed_values.0x00: bypass ON for Out8 HP with fixed frequency raw 0x000D
observed_values.0x01: observed for one HP slope endpoint
observed_values.0x12: previously observed in an Out8 HP mode run before the full slope series was measured
observed_values.0x13: observed for InA HP bypass-toggled counterpart
observed_values.0x14: observed for one HP slope endpoint and as the top of the fully measured Out8 HP slope/family run

mode_or_state_u8.note: The crossover HP mode byte is now treated as a common selector model for inputs and outputs: raw 0x00 means bypass ON and raw 0x01..0x14 form the slope/family enum run in crossover_slopes order. Older isolated InA 0x13 captures are treated as stale or context-dependent.

all_outputs_high_pass_slope_enum_mapping.basis: fully observed on Out8 and transferred to Out1..Out7 because output crossover write structure is otherwise identical except for the linear output channel byte

all_outputs_high_pass_bypass_mapping.basis: directly observed on Out8 after full slope decode and now treated as the common crossover rule for all channels

all_inputs_high_pass_slope_enum_mapping.basis: user confirmed that input crossover selectors follow the same pattern as outputs; channel byte changes, selector model stays the same

all_inputs_high_pass_bypass_mapping.basis: user confirmed input crossover uses the same selector model as outputs

earlier crossover runs were accidentally performed on Out8; those showed the same command structure with channel byte 0x0B
for InA Low Pass Frequency, payload 00 01 05 31 00 00 00 00 was observed
for InA Low Pass Bypass, payload 00 01 05 31 00 00 00 13 was observed
the InA Low Pass slope click did not produce a distinct write from the captured baseline, so input-side LP slope remains undecoded on the write side

Out8 Low Pass bypass was then toggled from OFF to ON while the selected slope remained fixed, and the write became 00 01 05 31 0B 2C 01 00; this establishes raw mode 0x00 as bypass ON for Out8 LP

the user later confirmed that input crossover selectors follow the same write-side pattern as the outputs; the crossover model is therefore treated as common for InA..InD and Out1..Out8, with only the channel byte changing

because all channels share the same command structure and only the channel index byte changes, the fully measured crossover LP selector pattern is treated as the common write rule for InA..InD and Out1..Out8: raw 0x00 means bypass ON and raw 0x01..0x14 map to crossover_slopes in enum order

frequency_u16.note: bytes 5 and 6 changed as a little-endian value during controlled LP frequency changes and fit the same logarithmic 19.7 Hz .. 20.16 kHz model used for PEQ frequency

observed_values.0x00: bypass ON for Out8 LP with fixed frequency raw 0x012C
observed_values.0x13: observed for InA LP bypass-toggled counterpart

mode_or_state_u8.note: The crossover LP mode byte is now treated as a common selector model for inputs and outputs: raw 0x00 means bypass ON and raw 0x01..0x14 form the slope/family enum run in crossover_slopes order. Older isolated InA 0x13 captures are treated as stale or context-dependent.

all_outputs_low_pass_slope_enum_mapping.basis: fully observed on Out8 and transferred to Out1..Out7 because output crossover write structure is otherwise identical except for the linear output channel byte

high_pass_slope_or_mode.note: same storage byte as high_pass_bypass, confirming that slope/family/bypass are combined in one mode/state byte

read.status_note: InA crossover is practically complete for decoding purposes except for low-pass slope/family storage. High-pass frequency, high-pass mode byte, low-pass frequency, and low-pass bypass are anchored.

high_pass_mode.note: The write-side selector for Out8 High Pass was later measured completely from 0x01 through 0x14 and matches crossover_slopes in order. This storage byte should be treated as HP slope/family mode, not as a simple bypass-only flag.

low_pass_mode.note: The write-side selector for Out8 Low Pass was later measured completely from 0x01 through 0x14 and matches crossover_slopes in order. This storage byte should be treated as LP slope/family mode, not as a simple bypass-only flag.

display_unit_selector.note: Current protocol evidence suggests the delay unit selector is a global GUI/display selector, not a per-channel stored field.

observations: InA delay fader in ms mode sent 00 01 04 38 00 00 FF
Out8 delay fader in ms mode sent 00 01 04 38 0B 00 FF
The payload layout matches the already known channel-indexed single-u16 write model used by other per-channel parameters.
Observed captures anchor the first and last channel indices and strongly support the full linear channel map 0x00..0x0B.

predicted_all_channels.rule: Delay value uses the same 0x38 payload layout for all 12 channels with only the channel byte changing linearly from 0x00 to 0x0B.

observations: automated delay read hypothesis test wrote InA from 0.0 ms to 20.0 ms and changed only block 0x03 offsets 7..8
automated delay read hypothesis test wrote InD from 0.0 ms to 20.0 ms and changed only block 0x0B offsets 27..28
automated delay read hypothesis test wrote Out1 from 0.0 ms to 20.0 ms and changed only block 0x0D offsets 31..32
automated delay read hypothesis test wrote Out8 from 0.0 ms to 20.0 ms and changed only block 0x1C offsets 9..10

full_channel_read_map.rule: Delay storage is strongly predicted to sit immediately after phase for each channel, i.e. gain +2 bytes = phase, and phase +2 bytes = delay. This matches the fully observed output delay map and the already proven gain/phase storage layout.

InA.basis: observed by automated delay read hypothesis test: writing InA from 0.0 ms to 20.0 ms changed only block 0x03 offsets 7..8; also matches gain@02:53 and phase@03:5 adjacency

InB.basis: predicted from InB gain@05:43 and phase@05:45

InC.basis: predicted from InC gain@08:33 and phase@08:35

InD.basis: observed by automated delay read hypothesis test: writing InD from 0.0 ms to 20.0 ms changed only block 0x0B offsets 27..28; also matches gain@0B:23 and phase@0B:25 adjacency

unit_selector_readback.note: The unit selector is observed as a global write command (0x15), but its persisted readback location in the block dump is not yet proven.

InA gate release fader changed only payload offsets 7 and 8 with command 0x3E and channel byte 0x00

InD gate release fader changed only payload offsets 7 and 8 with command 0x3E and channel byte 0x03

predicted_all_inputs.rule: Gate uses the same 0x3E payload field layout for InA..InD with only the input channel byte changing linearly from 0x00 to 0x03.

predicted_examples_by_field.note: These examples are write-side channel predictions derived from the observed InA and InD field layout. They should not be used as proof of complete raw-to-gui scaling for every parameter.

attack.note: controlled write diff with 00 01 0A 3E 00 00 00 63 00 63 00 00 00 vs 00 01 0A 3E 00 E6 03 63 00 63 00 00 00 changed bytes 0x1D..0x1E; u16le candidate at offset 29 changed from 0 to 998

release.note: controlled write diff with 00 01 0A 3E 00 00 00 00 00 09 00 00 00 vs 00 01 0A 3E 00 00 00 B7 0B 09 00 00 00 changed bytes 0x1F..0x20; u16le candidate at offset 31 changed from 0 to 2999. A later unique-pattern scan also hit the same value in unrelated positions, so only the targeted Gate offset is treated as the confirmed mapping.

hold.note: controlled write diff with 00 01 0A 3E 00 00 00 63 00 09 00 00 00 vs 00 01 0A 3E 00 00 00 63 00 E6 03 00 00 changed bytes 0x21..0x22; u16le candidate at offset 33 changed from 9 to 998

threshold.note: controlled write diff with 00 01 0A 3E 00 00 00 63 00 63 00 00 00 vs 00 01 0A 3E 00 00 00 63 00 63 00 B4 00 changed byte 0x23; u16le candidate at offset 35 changed from 0 to 180

attack.note: controlled write diff with 00 01 0A 3E 03 00 00 63 00 63 00 00 00 vs 00 01 0A 3E 03 E6 03 63 00 63 00 00 00 changed bytes 0x31..0x32 in block 0x08; u16le candidate at offset 49 changed from 0 to 998

threshold.note: controlled write diff with 00 01 0A 3E 03 00 00 63 00 63 00 00 00 vs 00 01 0A 3E 03 00 00 63 00 63 00 B4 00 changed byte 0x05 in block 0x09; u16le candidate at offset 5 changed from 0 to 180

full_input_read_map.rule: Gate read storage follows the same per-input stride pattern already observed for other input parameters: +3 blocks and -10 payload offsets per channel step, with wrap into the preceding block when needed. A unique-pattern dump scan matched the expected Gate values exactly at the predicted positions for InA..InD.

read.not_yet_proven: whether all gate read fields are stored as full u16le across the full value range

observations: Out1 limiter attack series with command 0x3F changed only payload offsets 5 and 6 and used channel byte 0x04

observations: Out1 -> InA was observed as 00 01 03 3A 04 01
Out1 -> InB was observed as 00 01 03 3A 04 02
Out1 -> InC was observed as 00 01 03 3A 04 04
Out2 -> InA was observed as 00 01 03 3A 05 01
Out8 -> InA was observed as 00 01 03 3A 0B 01
Out8 -> InC was observed as 00 01 03 3A 0B 04
write.inference: Matrix routing write-side currently looks like a single selected input per output encoded as an input bit mask, not a freely accumulated multi-input write model. The full write map above is predicted from the observed linear output channel mapping Out1..Out8 => 0x04..0x0B and input bit mapping InA..InD => 0x01/0x02/0x04/0x08.

predicted_full_read_map.rule: Matrix routing read follows the same per-output stride family as output matrix/crossover storage. The route byte appears immediately before the four matrix crosspoint gain u16 values for the output.

observations: Out1 routing changed block 0x0B offset 39 from 0x01 to 0x02 when written from InA to InB
Out8 routing changed block 0x1A offset 17 from 0x01 to 0x04 when written from InA to InC
the route byte sits immediately before the four matrix crosspoint gain u16 values for the same output
Out1 <- InB gain write observed as 00 01 05 41 04 01 17 01
Out1 <- InB gain write observed as 00 01 05 41 04 01 0E 01
Out1 <- InA gain write observed as 00 01 05 41 04 00 0E 01
Out8 <- InD gain write observed as 00 01 05 41 0B 03 0E 01
Out1 <- InA gain at -20.0 dB was observed as 00 01 05 41 04 00 50 00 (raw 80)
Out1 <- InA gain at -60.0 dB was observed as 00 01 05 41 04 00 00 00 (raw 0)
Out1 <- InB gain at -28.0 dB was observed as 00 01 05 41 04 01 40 00 (raw 64)
Out1 <- InB gain at -30.0 dB was observed as 00 01 05 41 04 01 3C 00 (raw 60)
The GUI showed the scripted all-crosspoint test pattern correctly for all outputs: InA=0.0 dB, InB=-1.0 dB, InC=-20.0 dB, InD=-30.0 dB
write.inference: Matrix crosspoint gain uses input index (0=InA,1=InB,2=InC,3=InD) rather than input bit mask. Gain value is a u16le at bytes 6..7. The exact raw-to-dB curve is not fully decoded yet; current observations securely establish several anchor points including 0.0 dB => 280, -1.0 dB => 270, -20.0 dB => 80, -28.0 dB => 64, -30.0 dB => 60 and -60.0 dB => 0.

read.stored_values: same raw gain model as write-side
observations: Out1 InB changed block 0x0B offset 43 from raw 270 to 60 when written from -1.0 dB to -30.0 dB
Out5 InA changed block 0x14 offset 7 from raw 280 to 80 when written from 0.0 dB to -20.0 dB
Out4 InB changed block 0x12 offset 5 from raw 270 to 60 when written from -1.0 dB to -30.0 dB
Out3 InD changed block 0x10 offset 5 from raw 280 to 60 when written from 0.0 dB to -30.0 dB
Out1 pattern read on block 0x0B showed route byte followed by four matrix gain u16 values: 280, 270, 80, 60
read.inference: Matrix crosspoint gain read layout follows the routing byte with four consecutive u16le gain values in input order InA, InB, InC, InD. When the remaining bytes in the current block are exhausted, the sequence wraps into the next block. This wrap behavior was directly observed for Out3/InD and Out4/InB.

switching to WHITE NOISE produced 00 01 03 39 02 00

the 31 sine selector steps align with the standard third-octave-like list 20Hz..20KHz used by the GUI

value_models.lowpass: u16le raw using the same exponential 19.7Hz..20.16kHz / 0..300 mapping as DSP408 crossover/PEQ frequency

post_apply_cmd56_probe.result: all 12 observed 0x56 chunks remained FF-filled after the 0x4B apply capture

0x4C.name: fir_processing_mode_write

channel_mapping.predicted_outputs: Out1..Out8 use channel bytes 0x04..0x0B; endpoints Out1 and Out8 are observed, intermediate outputs are predicted from the standard contiguous DSP408 channel map.

0x4C.readback_observation: After setting Out1 to FIR, a full 0x27/0x24 config readback of blocks 0x00..0x1F matched the initial baseline byte-for-byte. This mode is therefore not proven to be stored in the normal config dump.

observed_external_impulse_upload.selector_current_best_meaning: InA/input-slot-0 after user clarified txt/csv import is available only for InA..InD

two_tap_sample.meaning: coefficient[0]=1.0, coefficient[1]=0.5; confirms float32 big-endian upload encoding

0x4F.name: external_fir_transfer_control
0x4F.observed_context: first non-meter command before the 0x4E external FIR coefficient chunk burst

0x56.name: fir_file_or_coefficient_chunk_read
0x56.observed_response_data_content: all 0xFF in first startup log; after external impulse upload, offset 0 returned little-endian float32 1.0 followed by zeros; after two-tap upload, offset 0 returned little-endian float32 1.0, 0.5, then zeros
0x56.hypothesis: Likely reads FIR file/coefficient or FIR display/state data for one selector or slot. The observed address/position sequence is not simple 52-byte paging, so the field may be a sample position, frequency bin, or FIR memory coordinate rather than a raw byte offset. The first log has no non-0xFF data bytes, so value encoding is not yet decoded.

0x5B.name: external_fir_name_write
0x5B.observed_context: last non-meter command after the 0x4E external FIR coefficient chunk burst

open_questions.question: Does the 0x56 selector byte address input channel InA..InD directly, or an input FIR slot/bank?
Are the 52 response bytes FIR coefficients, file content, transfer status, or a directory/chunk payload?
Which write command changes one channel's FIR file assignment/name table entry?
Where are FIR enable and bypass fields stored, separately from the now observed Out1 IIR/FIR mode write?

tooling_notes.note: The general readBlock() helper in the current Java toolchain is limited to 0x00..0x1C, but the first FIR408 startup log observed config reads through 0x1F. FIR scripts therefore use direct write("00 01 02 27 XX", 0x24) requests for blocks 0x1D..0x1F until the toolchain is deliberately generalized.

Scripts that inject read/probe payloads should establish a fresh proxy-side session with connect(); resetSession(); ensureSession(); handshake(); before sending active reads. Attaching to a stale GUI-ended session can log PC_TO_DSP frames without receiving DSP_TO_PC responses.
